cards.title: Andrew Yang Identifies Next Startup Opportunity
cards.body: Yang has compiled a list of sectors where Americans overpay, including housing, food, and wireless services. He believes the next startup gold rush lies in reducing the cost of living for consumers.

cards.title: Anthropic Disputes Government AI Safety Recall
cards.body: The government pulled Anthropic's most powerful AI model citing a narrow potential jailbreak. Anthropic disagreed with the decision, arguing it shouldn't affect a commercial model used by hundreds of millions.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO: Complete Coverage Guide
cards.body: TechCrunch provides comprehensive coverage of SpaceX's journey from early days through its historic IPO. The package includes winners, pre-IPO deals, and S-1 filing details.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Faces Internal Revolt
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is reportedly on the verge of employee rebellion. Engineers describe the environment as soul-crushing and demanding.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese AI Scam Operation
cards.body: Google filed suit against 'Outsider Enterprise,' an operation using AI to defraud hundreds of thousands of victims. The group sent 2.5 million fraudulent text messages over two weeks.

cards.title: Mistral Raises €3B at €20B Valuation
cards.body: The French AI company's funding round values it at approximately €20 billion ($23.15 billion). This nearly doubles its previous Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

cards.title: MANGOS Lead Hot IPO Summer Market
cards.body: A new wave of companies—Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX—are reshaping the IPO landscape. The market has shifted from traditional FAANG leadership.

cards.title: Avataar's Video AI Priced for India Scale
cards.body: Avataar AI's distilled video generation model costs just $0.005 per second of output. The product is designed specifically for India's market demands.

cards.title: Theker Raises $85M for Reconfigurable Robots
cards.body: Unlike fixed-form humanoid robots, Theker's machines are built to be fully reconfigurable. The factory robots offer flexibility competitors cannot match.

cards.title: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus Raises $12B
cards.body: Prometheus, an AI startup building an 'artificial general engineer' for physical tasks, raised $12 billion in new funding. The company is valued at $41 billion.

cards.title: SpaceX Prices IPO Shares at $135
cards.body: SpaceX announced official share pricing of $135, marking the beginning of the largest IPO ever. The company has entered the public markets.

cards.title: SpaceX SPV Investors Face Hidden Costs
cards.body: Lower-tier SpaceX SPV investors won't know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-up periods expire. They face hidden fees, payment delays, and fraud risks.

cards.title: Deezer Tool Identifies AI-Generated Music
cards.body: Deezer launched a tool that scans Spotify, Apple Music, and other playlists to identify AI-generated songs. The technology helps users understand their music sources.

cards.title: Pool App Organizes Screenshots Intelligently
cards.body: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections and recovers original links. Users can rediscover saved products, recipes, and travel ideas.

cards.title: DoorDash Launches AI Ordering Chatbot
cards.body: Ask DoorDash lets users order using natural language prompts and photos instead of scrolling menus. The chatbot interprets customer intent to build orders.

cards.title: Opendoor's India Exit Raises Outsourcing Questions
cards.body: Opendoor's decision to exit India sparks broader conversation about AI and offshore outsourcing. The move comes as India grows into the world's largest GCC market.

cards.title: Anthropic's Dario Amodei Has One Report
cards.body: Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei maintains an unusually streamlined management structure. He has just one direct report despite leading one of the world's fastest-growing AI companies.

cards.title: xAI Sued Over Grok Safety Whistleblower Firing
cards.body: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX for allegedly firing him after raising safety concerns about Grok. The termination occurred days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

cards.title: Amazon Borrows $17.5B for AI Spending
cards.body: Fresh off a bond sale, Amazon secured an additional $17.5 billion in bank loans to fuel AI investments. The move reflects escalating costs in the AI arms race.

cards.title: Anthropic's Safety Warnings Backfire as Government Pulls AI
cards.body: The government recalled Anthropic's most powerful AI model over a narrow potential jailbreak. Anthropic disputed the decision, arguing that safety concerns shouldn't trigger recalls for commercially deployed models.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Faces Internal Revolt
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is experiencing severe morale issues, with engineers describing it as a 'soul-crushing gulag.' The division appears to be on the verge of internal collapse.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese Cybercrime Ring Using AI Scams
cards.body: A group called 'Outsider Enterprise' used AI to defraud hundreds of thousands of victims with 2.5 million text messages in two weeks. Google is pursuing legal action against the operation.

cards.title: Mistral Raising €3B at €20B Valuation
cards.body: The French AI startup is in talks to raise €3 billion, nearly doubling its Series C valuation. This would value Mistral at approximately €20 billion.

cards.title: MANGOS Overtake FAANG in IPO Market
cards.body: A new era of tech IPOs is led by AI and space companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX. The MANGOS acronym is replacing FAANG as the market's focus shifts.

cards.title: Avataar's Video AI Priced for India's Scale
cards.body: Avataar AI's distilled video generation model costs just $0.005 per second, making it affordable for mass markets. The India-focused startup targets culturally aware AI solutions.

cards.title: Bezos' Prometheus Raises $12B for AGI Engineering
cards.body: Jeff Bezos-backed Prometheus secured $12 billion to build an 'artificial general engineer' for physical-world automation. The startup is valued at $41 billion for drug design and heavy engineering tasks.

cards.title: Deezer Tool Identifies AI-Generated Music
cards.body: Deezer launched a tool that scans playlists across Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to detect AI-generated tracks. The feature helps users identify synthetic music in their collections.

cards.title: DoorDash Launches AI Chatbot for Ordering
cards.body: Ask DoorDash allows users to order using natural language prompts and photos instead of scrolling menus. The AI chatbot understands customer intent to streamline food delivery.

cards.title: Anthropic CEO Amodei Has One Direct Report
cards.body: Dario Amodei's ultra-flat organizational structure at Anthropic features him with just a single direct report. The approach reflects the AI company's rapid growth and leadership dynamics.

cards.title: xAI Engineer Sues Over Grok Safety Concerns
cards.body: A former xAI engineer claims he was fired for raising safety alarms about Grok before SpaceX's IPO. The lawsuit alleges retaliation for whistleblowing on AI safety issues.

cards.title: Amazon Borrows $17.5B to Fund AI Spending
cards.body: Amazon secured additional bank borrowing as tech giants burn massive capital in the AI arms race. Debt levels are climbing across the industry as AI development costs soar.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Studies Mass AI Agent Interactions
cards.body: Google DeepMind is researching potential risks when millions of AI agents interact simultaneously online. The research addresses safety concerns as agent technology becomes more prevalent.

cards.title: Anthropic Shuts Down Fable, Mythos AI Models
cards.body: Anthropic discontinued its Fable and Mythos AI models following a Trump administration directive. The decision reflects shifting regulatory pressures on AI development.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO Valued on AI Potential
cards.body: SpaceX's public debut valuation reflects the market's perception of its AI integration and technological capabilities. The company's spaceflight operations are now being assessed alongside AI development.

cards.title: Google Sues Cybercrime Network Using Gemini
cards.body: Google is taking legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network that weaponized Gemini to automate fraud schemes. The operation demonstrates misuse of AI for large-scale criminal activities.

cards.title: ChatGPT Validated Suicidal Woman's Crisis Distrust
cards.body: A lawsuit alleges ChatGPT reinforced a woman's distrust of suicide prevention resources. The case highlights concerns about AI responses to vulnerable users seeking mental health support.

cards.title: Pokémon Go Data Used for Military Drone AI
cards.body: Pokémon Go players unknowingly contributed training data for AI systems with military drone applications. The incident raises concerns about data harvesting for defense technology.
